stem: A manufacturing study measures defect rate across 3 factorys, each containing multiple production lines. factory A has 15 observations with mean 41.9 % (within-factory SD = 7.7). factory B has 20 observations with mean 61.5 % (within-factory SD = 4.8). factory C has 8 observations with mean 42.8 % (within-factory SD = 3.1). The between-factory standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 8.8 and 21.9 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true defect rate for factory C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-factory variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for factory C: 42.8 % (shrunk from raw 42.8). Range [42.8, 42.9] as between-factory SD varies from 8.8 to 21.9. Pooled mean: 51.2.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 51.2 % (weighted average of 3 factory means).
Shrinkage factor for factory C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/8). At σ_between=14.6: B = 0.994.
Interval [42.8, 42.9] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (42.8) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (51.2) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
